objective: The single hero Pitch Nav ad: establish the core product promise (a slow-motion video reveals what full speed hides) with premium, restrained pacing, then convert to a paid analysis.
audience: Broad primary audience — competitive pitchers 13-18 and their parents, plus pitching coaches evaluating the product for their athletes.
hook: THIS LOOKS LIKE A GOOD DELIVERY…
coreInsight: A delivery can look sound at full speed while the upper half is already rotating before the front side becomes stable — visible only once the video is stopped at foot strike.
openLoop: "This looks like a good delivery…" sets an expectation the very next line undercuts — the viewer keeps watching to see what the freeze frame reveals.
payoff: The freeze frame and overlay reveal the pattern; a second rep proves it's repeatable; the dashboard shows Pitch Nav surfaces exactly this kind of detail automatically.

scenes.cameraDirection: Real full-speed side-view pitch, no logo animation, no slow build.
onScreenText: THIS LOOKS LIKE A GOOD DELIVERY…
scenes.soundDesign: Single low tick on the cut in; music bed starts quiet.

assetReferences.description: Full-speed side-view pitch, subject A, rep 1

scenes.visualBeat: Visual proof continues, tension builds toward the freeze
scenes.cameraDirection: Same rep continues at full speed, then eases into slow motion as it approaches foot strike.
scenes.soundDesign: Speed-ramp whoosh as slow motion begins.

assetReferences.description: Same rep, slow-motion capture approaching foot strike

scenes.visualBeat: Freeze at the key phase, reveal the pattern
scenes.cameraDirection: Hard freeze at foot strike. Blue overlay animates onto the shoulders/hips to highlight the rotation-vs-stability pattern.
scenes.voiceOver: At full speed, this looks normal. But when we stop it at foot strike, the upper half begins rotating before the front side becomes stable.
scenes.soundDesign: Freeze-frame stop accent; overlay draw-on sound, subtle; music dips under VO.

assetReferences.description: Blue overlay highlighting the rotation-vs-stability pattern
assetReferences.notes: Motion-graphic overlay only — pitcher footage underneath is real.

scenes.visualBeat: Curiosity loop — second rep confirms the pattern
scenes.cameraDirection: Cut to a second rep, same freeze-and-overlay treatment at the same phase.
scenes.soundDesign: Freeze-frame stop accent, second instance, slightly harder.

assetReferences.description: Full-speed + slow-motion side-view pitch, subject A, rep 2

scenes.cameraDirection: Screen recording: dashboard and report, moving through priority, drills, and progress tracking — not a static frame.
scenes.voiceOver: Pitch Nav turns your slow-motion pitching video into a clear mechanics breakdown, a development priority, drills, and progress tracking.
scenes.soundDesign: UI tap accents, sparing; music builds slightly toward the final card.

assetReferences.description: Dashboard + report + priority + drills + progress calendar walkthrough

scenes.cameraDirection: Static end card: wordmark small at top, headline, CTA, price, and small-print disclaimer beneath.
onScreenText: Video-based training estimates. Not medical or laboratory measurements.
scenes.voiceOver: Understand your delivery. Start your Pitch Nav analysis. Twenty-five dollars a month.

voiceOverScript: At full speed, this looks normal. But when we stop it at foot strike, the upper half begins rotating before the front side becomes stable. Pitch Nav turns your slow-motion pitching video into a clear mechanics breakdown, a development priority, drills, and progress tracking. Understand your delivery. Start your Pitch Nav analysis. Twenty-five dollars a month.
onScreenText: THIS LOOKS LIKE A GOOD DELIVERY…
Video-based training estimates. Not medical or laboratory measurements.
bRollInstructions: Two reps from the same athlete, matched phase-to-phase, same freeze point (foot strike) both times. Dashboard footage must be a real, moving screen recording — not a static screenshot — and must show the priority, drills, and progress-tracking views the VO names.
editingNotes: This ad uses a slightly slower 6-beat structure than the standard 30s scaffold (see content/video-templates/timelines/30s.md) — the freeze-and-reveal is the entire point, so it's allowed to breathe for up to 6 seconds instead of the usual 5. Even so, something changes on screen at least every 1-3 seconds within each beat (overlay draw-on, split-second holds, UI taps). Open on the pitch, never the logo. The disclaimer must be legibly sized, not buried — see content/captions/caption-style-guide.md for minimum readable size at mobile scale.
soundDesign: Music bed under everything, ducked below voice-over, building slightly toward the final card. One whoosh on the speed ramp, two freeze-frame stop accents, sparing UI taps. No glitch transitions, no screen shake.

assetReferences.description: Subject A, two reps, full-speed + slow-motion, side view, matched to foot strike
Dashboard/report walkthrough: priority, drills, progress calendar
Blue overlay highlighting the mechanical pattern
Premium, restrained sports-tech bed, no lyrics, slight build toward the end card
